name: Northern-Style Lasagna
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook bacon in a 10-inch skillet over medium-high heat until crisp. Remove and set aside.
2. Add beef and onion to the skillet; cook until meat is browned and no pink remains, stirring once during cooking to separate the meat. Remove and discard any excess fat.
3. Stir in spaghetti sauce; set aside.
4. In a small bowl, combine soup and milk.
5. In another small bowl, mix ricotta with 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese.
6. In a 13x9-inch baking dish, spread about 1-1/2 cups of meat sauce.
7. Top with three lasagna noodles, spread half of the soup mixture, half of the cheese mixture, and one-third of the remaining meat sauce.
8. Repeat layers: top with remaining lasagna noodles and remaining meat sauce.
9. Cover with foil; bake at 375°F for 30 minutes.
10. Remove foil, sprinkle remaining Parmesan over the top, and bake an additional 10 minutes or until hot and bubbling. Let stand for 10 minutes before serving.
TIP: In northern Italy, many lasagna recipes often use a creamy white sauce along with the traditional red sauce.
